title: 1.1: Tests that PutBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 204 No Content when given a valid policy JSON with proper credentials
1.2: Tests that PutBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 400 Bad Request when content length exceeds maximum allowed policy size
1.3: Tests that PutBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 411 Length Required when content length is zero
1.4: Tests that PutBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 400 Bad Request when request body reader is nil
1.5: Tests that PutBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 403 Forbidden when credentials (access key and secret key) are empty
1.6: Tests that PutBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 400 Bad Request when policy JSON is malformed or invalid
1.7: Tests that PutBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 400 Bad Request when policy resource ARN bucket name differs from request bucket name
1.8: Tests that PutBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 404 Not Found when targeting a non-existent bucket
1.9: Tests that PutBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 400 Bad Request when bucket name has invalid format (e.g., starts with a dot)
1.10: Tests that PutBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 400 Bad Request when policy Version field is empty
1.11: Tests that GetBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 200 OK with the correct policy content when given valid credentials and an existing bucket with policy
1.12: Tests that GetBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 404 Not Found for non-existent bucket
1.13: Tests that DeleteBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 204 No Content when deleting policy from existing bucket with valid credentials
1.14: Tests that DeleteBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 404 Not Found for non-existent bucket
1.16: Tests that handlers return access denied for anonymous/unsigned HTTP requests
1.17: Tests that GetBucketPolicy handler returns HTTP 400 Bad Request when bucket name has invalid format
2.1: Places the test for PutBucketPolicy handler in cmd/bucket-policy-handlers_test.go
2.2: Places the test for GetBucketPolicy handler in cmd/bucket-policy-handlers_test.go
2.3: Places the test for DeleteBucketPolicy handler in cmd/bucket-policy-handlers_test.go
3.1: All tests use Go testing framework with *testing.T parameter and t.Fatalf/t.Errorf for assertions
3.2: All tests follow the naming pattern Test<HandlerName> for exported wrapper and test<HandlerName> for internal implementation function
3.3: All tests execute across both erasure and single-node setups (e.g., via ExecObjectLayerAPITest)
3.4: Tests use a clear, maintainable structure (e.g., table-driven tests or t.Run subtests) to cover multiple cases
4.1: Captures and asserts HTTP responses using standard httptest tooling (e.g., httptest.NewRecorder or httptest.Server)
4.2: Validates anonymous request handling using the suite’s anonymous-testing pattern (e.g., ExecObjectLayerAPIAnonTest) or an equivalent two-phase check
4.3: Includes a test for handler behavior when objectLayer is nil (e.g., via ExecObjectLayerAPINilTest)
4.4: Includes numbered test case comments (Test case - N) for each entry in the testCases slice
